spec_match.rationale: Runs a real VP8 VideoEncoder/VideoDecoder round-trip with source VideoFrame rotation/flip and configures the decoder from the encoder-provided decoderConfig, preserving the orientation metadata in the decoded VideoFrame.

interactive.rationale: Source rotation, boolean flip, and the encode/decode/inspect button drive an async WebCodecs pipeline with visible byte counts, decoder configuration status, decoded metadata, and render result.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page now describes a carriage check rather than assuming every backend serializes metadata; the live log reports whether metadata was preserved or a sidecar fallback was needed.

edge_cases.rationale: Handles missing WebCodecs or unsupported VP8 gracefully, shows coded/display dimensions on the decoded frame, links to the codec comparison page, and delegates active-orientation lock behavior to the sibling demo.

references.rationale: See-also includes ChromeStatus, the WebCodecs rotation spec anchor, the constructor demo, and the codec-carriage sibling.

design_system.rationale: CSS uses design-system variables; raw colors are confined to bitmap drawing.

summary: Verified in Chrome 148 with chrome-devtools-mcp: with rotation=90 and flip=true, the page encoded one VP8 chunk, used the encoder decoderConfig, decoded rotation=90 flip=true with display 320x240, and produced no console errors.
